name: The Fealy Law Firm, PC
description_short: Houston-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7, 13, and business bankruptcy with 30+ years of experience and board certification.
description_long:
The Fealy Law Firm, PC is a Houston-based bankruptcy law practice led by Vicky Fealy, a board-certified bankruptcy attorney with over 30 years of legal experience. The firm has provided legal assistance to thousands of Houston residents and businesses navigating financial hardship. They operate from a Houston office and offer free initial consultations to prospective clients.

The firm provides comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 liquidation bankruptcy for individuals and businesses, Chapter 13 restructuring bankruptcy with 3-5 year repayment plans, and Chapter 11 business bankruptcy. They also offer related debt relief services including wage garnishment relief, creditor harassment defense, loan modifications, divorce and bankruptcy coordination, and direct creditor negotiation to help clients avoid bankruptcy filing when possible. Their website details specific debt situations they handle: credit card debt, personal loans, payday loans, judgments, medical bills, student loans, foreclosures, repossessions, and IRS problems.

The firm distinguishes itself through board certification in bankruptcy law, emphasizing their formal credentials and expertise. They highlight the personal nature of their practice, with messaging focused on helping "good people through hard times" and acknowledging that debt often results from circumstances beyond client control. They advertise that many essential assets like homes, retirement savings, vehicles, and furniture may be exempt from liquidation, and they claim to have strategies for business owners to remain operational during restructuring.

Limitations include limited transparency about fees (not disclosed on website), lack of detail about success rates or case outcomes, and no information about attorney credentials beyond Vicky Fealy's board certification. The website mentions a scholarship program but provides incomplete information. For potential clients outside Houston or unable to visit in person, there is no indication of remote consultation availability.
address: 1235 N Loop W Suite 1120, Houston, TX 77008

company_info.headquarters: 1235 N Loop W Suite 1120, Houston, TX 77008

pros: Board-certified bankruptcy specialist, indicating formal credentials and expertise verification
Over 30 years of combined experience specifically in bankruptcy law
Offers free initial consultations with no upfront cost obligation
Handles full spectrum of bankruptcy types (Chapter 7, 13, 11) plus related relief services
Emphasizes asset protection knowledge, explaining exemptions for homes, retirement, vehicles, and furniture
Claims ability to negotiate directly with creditors to avoid bankruptcy filing in some cases
Serves both individual and business clients with tailored strategies
cons: No fee structure, pricing, or cost information disclosed on website
Limited attorney information beyond Vicky Fealy; unclear if she is the only attorney or if firm has associates
No case results, success rates, or client outcome data provided
Website does not specify whether remote consultations are available for non-local clients
Incomplete scholarship program information; details cut off on website
best_for: Houston-area residents facing Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy who want board-certified representation
Small business owners seeking business reorganization or liquidation guidance
Individuals with wage garnishment, creditor harassment, or specific debts (medical, payday loans, IRS) requiring specialized bankruptcy strategies
diagnosis: The Fealy Law Firm is best for Houston-area residents and business owners seeking experienced, board-certified bankruptcy representation for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 filing, or those needing creditor negotiation to avoid bankruptcy. The primary caveat is that fee information is not disclosed online, and potential clients must contact the firm directly for pricing; additionally, there is no clear indication of remote service availability for clients outside the Houston area.
services: Chapter 7 bankruptcy filing (liquidation for individuals and businesses)
Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing (3-5 year restructuring plans)
Student loan debt management through bankruptcy
